[[Category:Aelaki grammar]]

Questions in [[Aelaki]] are formed through a combination of question [[Particles|particles]] and the [[Zero|grammatical zero]] number.

== Question particles ==
* '''Shu''' = what / general question
* '''Shushu''' = open discussion
* '''Shi''' = give a final answer

By default these indicate a simple question of "Is this clause true?"

== General interrogative ("Did") ==
The simplest question adds '''Shu''' to a statement:

: '''Shabavadashro Roger Rabbit Shu'''
: "Did someone kill Roger Rabbit?"

== Interrogative zero marking (Who, what) ==
For questions about nouns, the noun in question is marked with [[Zero|zero number]]. Combined with the question particle, this transforms a negative into a question:

: '''Shafbavadashro Roger Rabbit'''
: "No man killed Roger Rabbit" (negative statement)

: '''Shafbavadashro Roger Rabbit Shu'''
: "Who killed Roger Rabbit?" (question)

This can be made more specific:

: '''Atufkunush Shafbavadashro Roger Rabbit Shu'''
: "What man killed Roger Rabbit?"

The zero number marks questions and can be applied to any part of the sentence. There is no animacy distinction that differentiates "who" from "what" in the way English does.

== Where, when, and why ==
These are formed with negated [[Converbs|converbs]] reflecting these concepts.

== How ==
Formed with a negation of the verb alongside the question particle. Different from "Did" — this assumes the event happened and asks about the method.

With [[Evidentialities|evidentials]] it changes meaning slightly. Most significant with:
* The [[Optative]]: "How is this thing good?"
* The [[Deliberative]]: "How am I questioning this?"

== See also ==
* [[Particles]]
* [[Zero]]
* [[Converbs]]
